steps.title: Use the graph intercepts
steps.content: The graph shows both lines sharing the $x$-intercept $(8,0)$, with positive $y$-intercepts near $(0,3.2)$ and $(0,6.4)$. This quickly eliminates systems whose intercepts do not match.

steps.title: Reject the wrong slopes
steps.content: Another nonmatching system has $x$-intercepts that do not match the graph, and its lines slope upward instead of downward. That does not fit the displayed lines.

steps.title: Check the shared intercept
steps.content: This system passes through the shared $x$-intercept $(8,0)$, but its $y$-intercepts are negative instead of positive. That puts the lines on the wrong side of the axis.

steps.title: Check the matching system
steps.content: For the remaining system, $4x+10y=32$ passes through $(8,0)$ and $(0,3.2)$, while $-8x-10y=-64$ passes through $(8,0)$ and $(0,6.4)$. Both match the graph.

steps.title: Select the system
steps.content: Only one system has the shared $x$-intercept, the two positive $y$-intercepts, and the downward slopes shown in the graph. The answer is D.
